title: Fox Reflects on US-Mexico Legacy at Texas Tribune Festival — 25 Years On
detail: Former President Vicente Fox participates in a virtual one-on-one interview at the Texas Tribune Festival, reflecting on Mexico-US relations, his collaboration with President George W. Bush on the landmark 2001 immigration framework, and the abrupt derailment of the 'whole enchilada' deal following the September 11 attacks. Speaking 25 years after his historic 2000 election that ended 71 years of PRI rule, Fox emphasizes the democratic transition as his presidency's defining achievement while expressing concern about Mexico's current security crisis under President Claudia Sheinbaum.

media.caption: Vicente Fox virtual interview at the Texas Tribune Festival, December 2025
